comment.body:
I can reproduce. The ads are not always at the start, example mpd: https://gist.github.com/ntnguyen1234/bbaaeb69eafc9b9fab82860f0737c066 (ads start at minute 8 or second 483).

However, we also need to not prune the live mpd content, due to this issue: https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/issues/182550#issuecomment-2223928344

This rule looks working for me, `/episode/` is used to avoid live ones (which contains `/channel/` at the URL instead):

```adblock
pluto.tv#%#//scriptlet('xml-prune', 'xpath(//*[name()="Period"][not(contains(@id,"subclip"))] | //*[name()="MPD"]/@mediaPresentationDuration | //*[name()="Period"]/@start)', '', '/\/episode\/.+?\.mpd\?/')
```

issue.title: www.jiji.com

labels.name: A: Problematic
labels.description: Issues, which cannot be resolved easily

issue.body:
# Sorry. The website is infected by AdShield, Inc.

Unfortunately, It is complicated to resolve with a regular adblock rule because the website is being protected by service of Ad-Shield that is an anti-adblock company.

## Userscript Installation
Please block Ad-Shield advertisement by installing [tinyShield userscript](https://github.com/FilteringDev/tinyShield).
Please check its installation guideline written in English by visiting [this URL](https://github.com/FilteringDev/tinyShield/blob/main/README.md).

If you can understand Korean language, please refer https://github.com/FilteringDev/tinyShield/blob/main/README.ko.md.

<details>
<summary>Detailed description for iOS users</summary>

1. Install [**Usercripts**](https://apps.apple.com/us/app/userscripts/id1463298887) app
2. Enable **Usercripts** extension in Safari settings
	* iOS 18+: `System settings` => `Apps` => `Safari` => `Extensions`
	* iOS 17 and older: `System settings` => `Safari` => `Extensions`
	Find **Usercripts**, enable it and allow `On other sites` permission
4. Open the [tinyShield ](https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/@filteringdev/tinyshield@latest/dist/tinyShield.user.js) userscript URL in browser
5. Press the extensions icon in the address bar of Safari and select Userscripts 
6. Tap to install
7. In opened popup, scroll down and press Install button
8. Done.

</details>

## Still Have a Problem?
An issue is occurred when the userscript is applied, please report to [this issue tracker](https://github.com/FilteringDev/tinyShield/issues).

comment.body:
@AdamWr Hi, on this site I want to match the script that has `html-load` as well, similar to

```adblock
#%#//scriptlet('trusted-replace-argument', 'HTMLScriptElement.prototype.setAttribute', '1', 'replace:/.+(error-report|html-load).+//g')
```

but I'm not sure how to adjust the current JS rule

```adblock
#%#//scriptlet('trusted-create-element', 'head', 'script', '', '(()=>{const t={apply:(t,e,r)=>{try{const t=r[1];t?.includes?.("error-report")&&(r[1]="")}catch(t){console.trace(t)}return Reflect.apply(t,e,r)}};window.HTMLScriptElement.prototype.setAttribute=new Proxy(window.HTMLScriptElement.prototype.setAttribute,t)})();')
```

issue.body:
# Sorry. The website is infected by AdShield, Inc.

Unfortunately, It is complicated to resolve with a regular adblock rule because the website is being protected by service of Ad-Shield that is an anti-adblock company.

## Userscript Installation
Please block Ad-Shield advertisement by installing [tinyShield userscript](https://github.com/FilteringDev/tinyShield).
Please check its installation guideline written in English by visiting [this URL](https://github.com/FilteringDev/tinyShield/blob/main/README.md).

If you can understand Korean language, please refer https://github.com/FilteringDev/tinyShield/blob/main/README.ko.md.

<details>
<summary>Detailed description for iOS users</summary>

1. Install [**Usercripts**](https://apps.apple.com/us/app/userscripts/id1463298887) app
2. Enable **Usercripts** extension in Safari settings
	* iOS 18+: `System settings` => `Apps` => `Safari` => `Extensions`
	* iOS 17 and older: `System settings` => `Safari` => `Extensions`
	Find **Usercripts**, enable it and allow `On other sites` permission
3. Open the [tinyShield ](https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/@filteringdev/tinyshield@latest/dist/tinyShield.user.js) userscript URL in browser
4. Press the extensions icon in the address bar of Safari and select Userscripts 
5. Tap to install
6. In opened popup, scroll down and press Install button
7. Done.

</details>

## Still Have a Problem?
An issue is occurred when the userscript is applied, please report to [this issue tracker](https://github.com/FilteringDev/tinyShield/issues).

comment.body:
This issue has been automatically marked as stale because it has been open for 3 days with no activity.
Unfortunately, our resources are limited and we can not always take the time to respond to requests about websites that are not popular enough. The issue will be closed if no further activity occurs.
Please note, that AdGuard Filters is an open-source project that is used by different content blockers and we welcome contributions. If you have some technical knowledge about how websites work, you are welcome to create a pull request following [these instructions](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).

comment.body:
> Sorry, does the above rule cause any issues on your side? I can see the app overlay if using ios simulator on desktop browser too

Yes, clicking is disabled when you chose "Add/Remove content disclosure" on your own post. The culprit was `x.com#$#div[role="group"] > div[data-testid="mask"] { display: none !important; }` but I disabled all the added rules as precaution which rather brought the issue. I think it's anyway safer to make the CSS rule conditioned, there had been issues on Outlook and Reddit by unconditional `overflow` rules. Does the `style` added to `html` include `overflow`?

issue.body:
# Sorry. The website is infected by AdShield, Inc.

Unfortunately, It is complicated to resolve with a regular adblock rule because the website is being protected by service of Ad-Shield that is an anti-adblock company.

## Userscript Installation
Please block Ad-Shield advertisement by installing [tinyShield userscript](https://github.com/FilteringDev/tinyShield).
Please check its installation guideline written in English by visiting [this URL](https://github.com/FilteringDev/tinyShield/blob/main/README.md).

If you can understand Korean language, please refer https://github.com/FilteringDev/tinyShield/blob/main/README.ko.md.

<details>
<summary>Detailed description for iOS users</summary>

1. Install [**Usercripts**](https://apps.apple.com/us/app/userscripts/id1463298887) app
2. Enable **Usercripts** extension in Safari settings
	* iOS 18+: `System settings` => `Apps` => `Safari` => `Extensions`
	* iOS 17 and older: `System settings` => `Safari` => `Extensions`
	Find **Usercripts**, enable it and allow `On other sites` permission
3. Open the [tinyShield ](https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/@filteringdev/tinyshield@latest/dist/tinyShield.user.js) userscript URL in browser
4. Press the extensions icon in the address bar of Safari and select Userscripts 
5. Tap to install
6. In opened popup, scroll down and press Install button
7. Done.

</details>

## Still Have a Problem?
An issue is occurred when the userscript is applied, please report to [this issue tracker](https://github.com/FilteringDev/tinyShield/issues).

comment.body:
Hi,
It seems that `trusted-replace-argument` is supported by all platforms for which that rule is necessary now, so I think that we can just remove `trusted-create-element` rule.
But this:
```adblock
#%#//scriptlet('trusted-create-element', 'head', 'script', '', '(()=>{const t={apply:(t,e,r)=>{try{const t=r[1];t&&/.+(error-report|html-load).+/.test(t)&&(r[1]="")}catch(t){console.trace(t)}return Reflect.apply(t,e,r)}};window.HTMLScriptElement.prototype.setAttribute=new Proxy(window.HTMLScriptElement.prototype.setAttribute,t)})();')
```
should works.

comment.body:
Может приложение использует свои DNS вместо системного? Или пользователь включает ВПН, у которого свой DNS?
У меня наш DNS используется на роутере, и вижу, что запросы к рекламе блокируются.
